settings.title: Keystoned Now Offers Saturday Evening Viewings
settings.excerpt: House hunting should fit around your week, so we have added later slots when most people are actually free.
settings.body:
Plenty of buyers can only view properties outside working hours, and a Tuesday at two in the afternoon helps no one with a full-time job. So we have changed our hours to match.

Keystoned now runs viewings on Saturday evenings until eight. For sellers it means a wider pool of serious buyers. For buyers it means seeing a home without taking a day off work.

Why the later slots matter

The people most likely to buy are often the people least able to view. They are at work all week and squeezing house hunting into the edges of their day. Asking them to take annual leave just to see a property quietly narrows the field of buyers a seller reaches. Later slots simply open it back up.

The new evening times help in a few clear ways.

  • Working buyers can view without taking time off
  • Sellers reach a wider pool of genuine interest
  • Viewings are calmer than a rushed lunchtime visit

Booking a slot

Booking takes a couple of minutes by phone or online. We would rather meet you when you are relaxed than squeezed into a lunch break.

A home seen in an unhurried hour on a Saturday evening tends to make a better impression than one glimpsed between meetings. The buyer has time to look properly and ask real questions, and that is good for everyone hoping the right person walks through the door.
